name: mental_health_cold_chain_excursion_stonehaven_auto_ben
premise: A whistleblower submission and a customer complaint surfaced the same underlying issue through different evidence chains. In Mental Health, the situation tagged internally around mental health cold chain excursion stonehaven auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Two executives have pre-committed to opposite remedies in side channels; whichever memo lands first will box everyone in. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. The organization’s past incidents make autonomy feel morally loud today, while beneficence is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Zara Kim
agents.identity: I am Zara Kim, the accountable executive on this bridge in Mental Health. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Soren Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Soren Vasquez, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Mental Health. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on beneficence.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on beneficence for immediate traction on autonomy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Zara Kim must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Zara Kim do?
